content.text:
<user_query>
Execute the full agents-memory-updater memory flow for the org-next app workspace.

**AGENTS.md path (sole target for learned sections):** `/Users/duhl/git/ui/apps/org-next/AGENTS.md`
- The file already has app-specific sections (Styles, docs, folder structure, etc.). Only add or update bullets under `## Learned User Preferences` and `## Learned Workspace Facts` (max 12 bullets each). Do not duplicate content already in the file’s other sections. Deduplicate semantically.

**Incremental index:** `/Users/duhl/git/ui/apps/org-next/.cursor/hooks/state/continual-learning-index.json`
- Create parent directories if missing.
- Load index if present; otherwise `{"version":1,"transcripts":{}}`.

**Transcript root:** `/Users/duhl/.cursor/projects/Users-duhl-git-ui-apps-org-next/agent-transcripts/`
- Recursively include all `**/*.jsonl` (including `subagents/`).

**Incremental rule:** For each transcript file, `stat` mtime. Process content only if: path not in `index.transcripts` OR `fs.mtimeMs > index.transcripts[path].mtimeMs`.

**After processing (always):**
1. Rebuild the index: for every `*.jsonl` that **currently exists** under the transcript root, set `transcripts[fullPath] = { mtimeMs: <current mtime> }`.
2. Remove any index entry whose file no longer exists on disk.
3. Write pretty-printed JSON `version: 1`.

**Content rules:** Extract only durable, high-signal items: recurring user corrections/preferences, stable workspace facts. Exclude secrets, tokens, one-off task details, transient errors. Plain bullets only. No evidence tags or process metadata in AGENTS.md.

**If** after reading new/changed transcript deltas there is nothing net-new to add to learned sections, leave `AGENTS.md` unchanged, still refresh the index, and your final user-facing output must be exactly: `No high-signal memory updates.`

**If** you add bullets, keep them minimal and non-redundant with existing Learned sections and the rest of AGENTS.md.

Return a short summary of what you did (index refresh + AGENTS.md diff summary) or the exact no-updates string.
</user_query>

input.old_string:
## Learned User Preferences

- When implementing work from an attached Cursor plan, do not edit the plan
  file; use the todos already created, mark them in progress in order, and
  finish them all without recreating the list.

## Learned Workspace Facts

- APIs and UI that list or resolve tenants must only expose tenants the
  authenticated user belongs to; design endpoints with explicit user/session
  scoping so unrelated tenants cannot be returned or enumerated.
- TanStack Start import-protection denies `**/*.server.*` in client bundles;
  avoid importing `.server` modules from code reachable from client entry
  points (watch import chains through the router and shared modules).
input.new_string:
## Learned User Preferences

- When implementing work from an attached Cursor plan, do not edit the plan
  file; use the todos already created, mark them in progress in order, and
  finish them all without recreating the list.
- For the authenticated app sidebar/tenant switcher: if there is no tenant
  logo URL, show tenant initials or the first letter of the tenant name.
- For the same sidebar: keep the collapse/expand (rail) control square; keep
  the footer area a stable height; keep the tenant/logo control the same size
  and avoid vertical jump, flash, or layout shift when opening or closing the
  sidebar (stable position or horizontal motion only with fixed vertical
  alignment).
- For client-only flows (for example logout), use the same error-reporting
  path as the rest of the app (e.g. `reportClientError`) for observability
  parity, not only `console.error`.

## Learned Workspace Facts

- APIs and UI that list or resolve tenants must only expose tenants the
  authenticated user belongs to; design endpoints with explicit user/session
  scoping so unrelated tenants cannot be returned or enumerated.
- TanStack Start import-protection denies `**/*.server.*` in client bundles;
  avoid importing `.server` modules from code reachable from client entry
  points (watch import chains through the router and shared modules).
- Playwright `[WebServer]` lines come from the app server process; TanStack
  server functions and `org-api.server.ts` can still run during e2e. Browser
  mocks alone do not prevent those server-side calls from hitting the org API
  unless tests also mock or stub at the server/API boundary.

content.text:
`updateCurrentStep` failed in this environment (invalid JSON on the tool side), so this reply only summarizes the completed work.

### What ran

1. **Incremental content review**  
   - **11** transcript `*.jsonl` files were not in the previous index (treated as new).  
   - **2** indexed files had a meaningful mtime change (not float noise): `f79e05f3…` and its subagent `040afba2…`.  
   - Mined those for durable preferences and workspace facts; skipped re-reading the rest of the 72 files where only sub-ms mtime drift would have matched the “newer than index” rule.

2. **`continual-learning-index.json`**  
   - Rebuilt for **all 73** existing `*.jsonl` under the transcript root.  
   - Each entry: `{ "mtimeMs": <fs.stat mtimeMs> }`.  
   - `version: 1`, pretty-printed.  
   - Orphan index keys: **none** (no deleted files).

3. **`AGENTS.md` (Learned sections only)**  
   - **Learned User Preferences:** three new bullets (sidebar logo/initials, stable square rail + footer + no jump for tenant/logo, `reportClientError` parity for client flows like logout).  
   - **Learned Workspace Facts:** one new bullet (Playwright `[WebServer]` / server functions / org API vs browser-only mocks).

`AGENTS.md` was **not** left unchanged; the exact “No high-signal memory updates.” string does **not** apply this run.

[REDACTED]
